text.title: Business Process Analysis Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example: invoice-approval process before/after.

list.item: 2 stakeholder interview transcripts (Maria Lopes AP, Pedro Silva CFO)
30-day ticket export from Jira AP project (CSV)

section.title: Stage 1 — identify
section.p: process_id: invoice-approval. Trigger: invoice email arrives. Exit: invoice posted in ERP.

section.title: Stage 2 — current state
section.code:
Lanes: - AP (Maria Lopes, AP Lead) - Finance (Pedro Silva, CFO) - IT (José Almeida, ERP Admin)
Steps: s-01 [AP] Receive email (source: ops/sop.md#L8) s-02 [AP] Manual entry into ERP form (source: ops/sop.md#L15) s-03 [Finance] Approval threshold check (> $5k) (source: interview-pedro#03:14) s-04 [Finance] Sign-off in DocuSign (source: ops/sop.md#L22) s-05 [AP] Re-key approval into ERP (source: interview-maria#08:40) s-06 [IT] Reconcile failed entries weekly (source: jira-ap#tickets-30d)

section.title: Stage 4 — future-state diff
section.code: | step | action | expected_delta | |------|--------|----------------| | s-01 | change | Mailgun webhook → -4 min, -$0.9 | | s-02 | cut | n8n parses + auto-creates ERP draft → -12 min, -$2.9 | | s-05 | cut | DocuSign webhook posts back to ERP → -9 min, -$2.1 | | s-06 | change | error-queue + Slack alert → -38 min, -$9.5 |

section.title: Stage 5 — signoffs
Outcome
section.p: Future-state cuts 63 minutes per invoice and $15.4 cost. Hand off future_state[] to automation team as the implementation contract.
